mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal improving labor market conditions and rising consumer income, which support corporate earnings and drive the S&P 500 higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has been rising consistently since early 2009
failure_condition: If PAYEMS experiences a monthly decline but SPX still posts positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of such instances, or if the average forward return following increases in PAYEMS is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near the zero lower bound (below 0.25%), the market anticipates prolonged accommodative monetary policy, which compresses the equity risk premium and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been below 0.25% since December 2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative average returns over 60 trading days following the first month FEDFUNDS drops below 0.25%, or if the cumulative return over the next quarter is negative in more than 3 out of 5 historical zero-lower-bound episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of ultra-low policy rates (FEDFUNDS near zero) and accelerating consumer spending (rising RSAFS) creates a powerful positive impulse for equities: low discount rates amplify the present value of rising cash flows from consumption-driven sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS has risen from 340,000 to 352,073 over last 3 months
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * RSAFS growth) does not show a statistically significant positive coefficient in a predictive regression for 20-day SPX returns (p>0.10), or if the signal's rank IC is below 0.03 over a 1-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession recovery phase, housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of durable demand; rising starts signal consumer confidence and construction activity, which predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has rebounded from 470 to 529 over recent months
failure_condition: If in the post-recession recovery regime (defined as period after the NBER trough date) when HOUST rises month-over-month, the S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative more often than positive (less than 50% signal accuracy), or if the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from its cyclical peak signals that the labor market is healing, which boosts consumer confidence and spending expectations, leading to higher equity prices over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE three-month rolling average declines by at least 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE peaked at 10.0% in October 2009 and now stands at 10.0% (stable)
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines by 0.2pp over three months but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of subsequent months, or if the average return following such declines is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate expanding factory output and supply capacity, which reaffirms the strength of the economic recovery and supports corporate profits, driving the S&P 500 higher over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been increasing since mid-2009, currently at 99.44
failure_condition: If the 3-month average return following months with INDPRO increase is not positive, or if the predictive rank correlation over 2-year rolling windows is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of low consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and near-zero policy rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'sweet spot' for equities: no imminent tightening threat while discount rates are minimal, amplifying equity valuations.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both CPIAUCSL below trend and FEDFUNDS below 1%) does not predict positive 20-day forward returns with at least 55% accuracy in out-of-sample testing, or if the average excess return is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS) relative to the previous month signals a surge in consumer demand that directly boosts revenues for consumer-facing firms, leading to immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS grew from 348,000 to 352,073 over recent months
failure_condition: If following RSAFS monthly growth >1%, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the average positive return is less than the average negative return in absolute value.
